title: 9分达人听力7 Test 2 Listening
source: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

groups.title: Accommodation for Countryside Holiday
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Holiday Details • Preferred location: $1$ • Try to avoid: $2$ • Length of stay: a $3$ Special Requirements • Must be $4$ • A good $5$ for children • A $6$ nearby Additional Requirements • No stairs • Prefer a $7$ instead of a balcony Personal Details • Name: Mrs $8$ • Contact number: $9$ • Source of information: the ads from the Magazine of $10$

questions.title: Preferred location: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 preferred、location，此处应填地点名词或名词短语。
题目解析：本题作为开篇第一题，在一段简短的背景介绍之后，通过男士提问 “Where are you going to spend your holiday?” 可以定位到该题，准备抓取地点信息。女士回答 “… last year we spent our holiday in the southern part of the city, but this time we'd like to live in the northeast”，可以判断 southern part（去年的度假地点）为干扰信息，northeast 为正确答案。

questions.title: Try to avoid: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 avoid，此处应填负向信息搭配的名词或动名词短语。
题目解析：本题定位信息不明显。上一题结束后，通过男士提问 “And what about the time of your holiday?” 可以判断话题的切换，并且定位到该题。接着在女士的回答中先后出现 first week、September、off-peak、time 等名词信息，但都未搭配负向信息。继续往下听，听到 “At peak season, the attractions are always crowded with people, and hotels or restaurants might overcharge you, so we try to avoid that.”，其中 avoid that 与题干信息一致，that 指代前文的 peak season，可确认 peak season 为正确答案。

questions.title: Length of stay: a 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 length、a，此处应填单数名词或名词短语，表示一段时间。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过男士提问 “Then how long are you going to stay?” 可以定位到该题。先听到女士提及时间信息 three days，但其是复数名词，不符合题干要求，可判断其为干扰信息。继续往下听，听到 we will stay there for a weekend，其中 weekend 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。

questions.title: Must be 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Special Requirements；提示词为 must be，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过男士提问中的 particular requirements 可以定位到该题，准备抓取答案词。女士回答 the first priority we've considered is, of course, a quiet environment，其中 quiet 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。

questions.title: A good 【   】 for children
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 a、good、children，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：本题定位信息不明显。上一题结束后，听到女士提到 “Then we'd prefer a cottage on a good beach where my children can play with great fun,”，其中 beach 前后搭配题干的 good 和 children，符合题干要求，记录下来即可。注意，虽然 cottage 的词性和形式符合题干要求，但其前后并未搭配 good 和 children 等题干信息，可判断其为干扰信息。

questions.title: A 【   】 nearby
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 a、nearby，此处应填单数名词或名词短语，表示某个地点。
题目解析：本题定位信息也不明显。上一题结束后，听到女士提到 another thing is，可以定位到该题，准备抓取地点信息。女士提到 “… I'd prefer a good restaurant not far away from where we live.”，其中 not far away from 对应题干的 nearby，替换难度不大，可以判断 restaurant 为正确答案。

questions.title: Prefer a 【   】 instead of a balcony
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 no stairs；提示词为 a、instead of、balcony，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上层信息 no stairs 出现后，可以定位到该题。男士提问 “Do you need a balcony?”，随即被女士否定 “… it's not necessary for us ...”，女士接着提到 we need a garden，可判断 garden 符合题干要求，记录下来即可。

questions.title: Name: Mrs 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词、提示词为 name，此处应填人名，一般会给出字母拼写。
题目解析：本题定位不难，通过男士提问 “What's your surname?” 可以定位到该题，准备抓取答案词。女士回答 “Cheffins, that's C-H-E-double F-I-N-S.”，根据字母拼写记录下来即可。注意 double 这个考点。

questions.title: Contact number: 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词、提示词为 contact number，此处应填电话号码。
题目解析：上一题结束后，女士提到 “And my telephone number is 0192477285.”，直接定位并记录数字即可。该串电话号码有 10 位数，需要对数字有一定的敏感度，建议对数字反应较慢的考生进行专项训练。

questions.title: Source of information: the ads from the Magazine of 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 ads、magazine；提示词为 magazine、of，此处应填杂志名称，词性无法准确预判。
题目解析：题干中的 ads 和 magazine 原词重现，随后女士提到 “… a magazine called Countryside Living”，可判断该杂志名称为 Countryside Living，记录下来即可。注意，答案词首字母小写也算正确，大小写不影响得分。

transcript.en: Jack: Great Countryside Holidays, Jack speaking.
transcript.cn: 您好，这里是美好乡村度假公司，我是杰克。

transcript.en: Jack: Can I help you?
transcript.cn: 有什么可以帮您的吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Yes, I hope so.
transcript.cn: 有的，希望您能帮上忙。

transcript.en: Woman: I've heard that your travel agency provides holidays in the countryside.
transcript.cn: 我听说你们旅行社提供乡村度假服务。

transcript.en: Jack: That's right.
transcript.cn: 是的。

transcript.en: Jack: We've specialized in countryside holidays with many years of experience, so we can ensure that we provide perfect holidays with several destinations.
transcript.cn: 我们专营乡村度假已有多年经验，因此能够确保在多个目的地为您提供完美的度假体验。

transcript.en: Woman: That's great!
transcript.cn: 太好了！

transcript.en: Woman: My husband and I are planning a countryside holiday with the whole family, and I'm calling just for the accommodation.
transcript.cn: 我和丈夫正计划带全家去乡村度假，我打电话来只是想咨询住宿。

transcript.en: Jack: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Jack: Where are you going to spend your holiday?
transcript.cn: 你们打算去哪里度假？

transcript.en: Jack: There're some excellent accommodations on our list in different destinations.
transcript.cn: 我们的名单上有不同目的地的一些优质住宿。

transcript.en: Woman: Well, last year we spent our holiday in the southern part of the city, but this time we'd like to live in the northeast.
transcript.cn: 嗯，去年我们在这座城市的南部度假，但这次我们想住在东北部。

transcript.en: Woman: I have a friend who's just returned and had great fun there.
transcript.cn: 我有个朋友刚从那里回来，玩得非常开心。

transcript.en: Jack: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Jack: And what about the time of your holiday?
transcript.cn: 那你们打算什么时候度假？

transcript.en: Woman: It'll be in the first week of September, which we think will be off-peak travel time.
transcript.cn: 会在九月的第一周，我们认为那时是旅游淡季。

transcript.en: Woman: At peak season, the attractions are always crowded with people, and hotels or restaurants might overcharge you, so we try to avoid that.
transcript.cn: 旺季时景点总是人满为患，酒店或餐馆也可能会多收费，所以我们尽量避开旺季。

transcript.en: Jack: Yes, arranging your holiday during off-peak time is wise as you can enjoy the attractions and facilities better.
transcript.cn: 是的，把假期安排在淡季很明智，因为你们可以更好地享受景点和设施。

transcript.en: Woman: That's right.
transcript.cn: 没错。

transcript.en: Jack: Then how long are you going to stay?
transcript.cn: 那么你们准备住多久？

transcript.en: Woman: Our holiday will last three days, and we will stay there for a weekend.
transcript.cn: 我们的假期会持续三天，会在那里度过一个周末。

transcript.en: Jack: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Jack: That's the location and time of the holiday.
transcript.cn: 度假的地点和时间就这些。

transcript.en: Jack: Then let's turn to the accommodation itself.
transcript.cn: 那么我们来谈谈住宿本身吧。

transcript.en: Jack: Do you have any particular requirements for housing?
transcript.cn: 你们对住房有什么特别要求吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Yes, the first priority we've considered is, of course, a quiet environment so that both young and old can have good rest during our stay.
transcript.cn: 有，我们首先考虑的当然是环境安静，这样无论老人还是孩子，入住期间都能好好休息。

transcript.en: Jack: No problem.
transcript.cn: 没问题。

transcript.en: Woman: Then we'd prefer a cottage on a good beach where my children can play with great fun.
transcript.cn: 另外，我们更想住在优质海滩旁的一栋小屋里，让孩子们可以尽情玩耍。

transcript.en: Woman: My little daughter loves collecting shells and building sandcastles.
transcript.cn: 我的小女儿很喜欢捡贝壳和堆沙堡。

transcript.en: Jack: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Jack: I'll take that down.
transcript.cn: 我会记下来的。

transcript.en: Jack: A seaside cottage is excellent.
transcript.cn: 海边小屋很不错。

transcript.en: Jack: It also means secluded nights and fresh sea air.
transcript.cn: 这也意味着夜晚幽静，还能呼吸清新的海风。

transcript.en: Woman: Right.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Woman: Oh, another thing is, I'm kind of a foodie, and whenever I visit a new place, I'm always desperate to try the local cuisine.
transcript.cn: 哦，还有一件事，我算是个美食爱好者，每到一个新地方，我总是特别想尝尝当地美食。

transcript.en: Woman: So if possible, I'd prefer a good restaurant not far away from where we live.
transcript.cn: 所以如果可以的话，我希望住处附近能有一家不错的餐馆。

transcript.en: Jack: No problem.
transcript.cn: 没问题。

transcript.en: Jack: Tasting the local cuisine is one of the delights of the holiday.
transcript.cn: 品尝当地美食是度假的乐趣之一。

transcript.en: Jack: Then what about the actual room?
transcript.cn: 那么具体的房间呢？

transcript.en: Jack: Are there any other requirements?
transcript.cn: 还有其他要求吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Our family has five members, so we'll need a cottage with three living rooms and a separate bathroom with a shower.
transcript.cn: 我们家有五口人，所以需要一栋有三间起居室的小屋，还要有一间带淋浴的独立浴室。

transcript.en: Jack: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Jack: Three living rooms and a separate bathroom with a shower.
transcript.cn: 三间起居室，以及一间带淋浴的独立浴室。

transcript.en: Woman: Oh, another important thing is, my father got his backache last week, and climbing stairs might cause trouble.
transcript.cn: 哦，还有一件重要的事，我父亲上周背痛，爬楼梯可能会有困难。

transcript.en: Woman: I'm concerned that he might not recover from it during our holiday, so there shouldn't be any stairs in the cottage, I'm afraid.
transcript.cn: 我担心度假期间他的背痛可能还没好，所以恐怕小屋里不能有楼梯。

transcript.en: Jack: No stairs.
transcript.cn: 不能有楼梯。

transcript.en: Jack: OK, I've taken that down.
transcript.cn: 好的，我已经记下来了。

transcript.en: Jack: Do you need a balcony?
transcript.cn: 你们需要阳台吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Actually, it's not necessary for us, but if possible, we need a garden, you know, where the children can play.
transcript.cn: 其实我们不需要阳台，但如果可以的话，我们需要一个花园，你知道的，可以让孩子们在那里玩。

transcript.en: Jack: No problem.
transcript.cn: 没问题。

transcript.en: Jack: Several cottages cater to your need.
transcript.cn: 有几栋小屋符合你们的需求。

transcript.en: Woman: Great!
transcript.cn: 太好了！

transcript.en: Woman: Well, that's all our needs for the accommodation.
transcript.cn: 嗯，这就是我们对住宿的全部要求。

transcript.en: Jack: OK, I've written down all the details.
transcript.cn: 好的，我已经把所有细节都记下来了。

transcript.en: Jack: And I'll contact you as soon as I find the one which can satisfy your needs.
transcript.cn: 我一找到能满足你们需求的住处，就会尽快联系您。

transcript.en: Woman: Thank you.
transcript.cn: 谢谢。

transcript.en: Jack: I'll need your personal details.
transcript.cn: 我需要您的个人信息。

transcript.en: Jack: What's your surname?
transcript.cn: 您姓什么？

transcript.en: Woman: Cheffins, that's C-H-E-double F-I-N-S.
transcript.cn: 切芬斯，拼写是 C-H-E、两个 F、I-N-S。

transcript.en: Jack: OK, Mrs Cheffins.
transcript.cn: 好的，切芬斯太太。

transcript.en: Woman: And my telephone number is 0192477285.
Jack: All right.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Jack: Before we finish, I'd like to know where you heard about us.
transcript.cn: 在结束之前，我想知道您是从哪里了解到我们的。

transcript.en: Jack: Did your friend tell you?
transcript.cn: 是您的朋友告诉您的吗？

transcript.en: Woman: Actually, not.
transcript.cn: 其实不是。

transcript.en: Woman: I saw the ads of your travel agency in a magazine called Countryside Living.
transcript.cn: 我在一本名为《乡村生活》的杂志上看到了你们旅行社的广告。

transcript.en: Jack: I see.
transcript.cn: 明白了。

transcript.en: Jack: That's a popular magazine.
transcript.cn: 那是一本很受欢迎的杂志。

transcript.en: Woman: Yes.
transcript.cn: 是的。

transcript.en: Woman: Thank you very much.
transcript.cn: 非常感谢。

transcript.en: Jack: My pleasure, Mrs Cheffins.
transcript.cn: 不客气，切芬斯太太。

transcript.en: Jack: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

groups.title: An Electronic Toy Company
groups.desc:
Questions 11-15
Choose the correct answer, A, B or C.

questions.title: What point is made about the history of the company?

options.title: B
options.content: It took a long time to establish.

options.title: C
options.content: It was set up by a relative.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词、提示词为 history。
题目解析：本题作为开篇第一题，在一段自我介绍后，听到定位词 history 原词重现，提示定位到该题。原文先提到 “We're quite an old company, specializing in electronic toys for more than thirty years.”，其中 thirty years 指的是公司生产电动玩具的时间长，而不是 B 选项“花了很长时间建立起来”。接着原文提到 “With the support of his grandfather, he got enough start-up capital ...”，表明公司是由亲戚资助的，而不是 C 选项说的 set up。最后原文提到 “… the company was set up and succeeded in a short period.”，其中 set up 对应 A 选项的 establish，in a short period 对应 quickly，可确认 A 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Why should the company recruit more temporary staff?

options.title: A
options.content: It plans to develop new products.

options.title: B
options.content: The old staff are having an illness.

options.title: C
options.content: It aims to meet the new demands of customers.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 temporary staff；提示词为 why、recruit、more。
题目解析：本题定位信息不明显。上一题结束后，原文提到 “To satisfy the demands of customers ...”，部分考生可能会误选 C 选项，不过原文随后接的信息是 the number of staff members has grown up on a regular basis，与题干的 recruit more temporary staff 不一致，可排除 C 选项。接着原文提到 “… we decided to introduce new electronic toys to give that a boost, so we have to look for more workers to help us, and that's the reason why you are here.”，其中 decided to introduce new electronic toys 对应 A 选项，more workers 对应题干的 more temporary staff，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。原文未提及 B 选项的 old staff 和 illness。

questions.title: How long will the employees work in the company?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 how long、employees、work。
题目解析：本题定位信息不明显。题干的 employee 指代原文的说话对象，可能会通过代词 you 来体现。上一题结束后，可根据提示词 how long 来抓取时间信息。原文先提到 the first week，但并非指工作时间，接着提到 “… you'll be expected to work here for six months,”，可知工作时间为六个月，对应 B 选项的 half a year，因此 B 选项为正确答案。之后原文还提到 definite answer，注意这里的 definite 表示“明确的”，与 C 选项 indefinitely（无限期地）意思相反，可排除该选项。

questions.title: The largest number of positions are in the department of
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 largest number。
题目解析：根据提示词 largest number，可判断本题需留意程度信号词，比如具体数值。上一题结束后，原文提到 seventy percent of whom are responsible for sending out goods，其中 seventy percent 是题干 largest number 的具象化体现，sending out goods 对应 C 选项的 package delivery，因此 C 选项为正确答案。接着原文提到 the minority of jobs are in the manufacturing line，其中 manufacturing 对应 B 选项的 production，minority 与题干的 largest number 相矛盾，可排除 B 选项。A 选项在原文未提及。

questions.title: What's the current problem with the company?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 current、problem。
题目解析：本题定位信息不明显。上一题结束后，原文先后提到 modern machinery 和 advanced skills，分别与 A、B 选项相矛盾，并且原文也未提及题干的 current problem，可排除 A、B 选项。定位信息通过转折词引出 “However, I must tell you one thing which is not so pleasant”，其中 not so pleasant 对应题干负向信息 problem，提示答案即将出现。随后听到 “… I'm afraid that you won't get paid on time this month.”，其中 won't get paid on time 对应 C 选项，题干的时间信号词 current 通过时态和 this month 体现出来，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。

groups.question_title: What's the responsibility of each temporary staff member?
groups.desc:
Questions 16-20
What's the responsibility of each temporary staff member?
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 16-20.

questions.title: 1st person
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 1st person。
题目解析：定位词 1st person 在答案句后出现，考生可通过 “There are five positions and you'll be working in different departments.” 这个总起句定位到该题。原文提到 “… we're in urgent need of someone who can deal with the salary problem ...”，其中 salary 对应 B 选项 wage，接着原文提到 “… that's the primary job for the first person.”，可确认 B 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: 2nd person
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 2nd person。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文随即提到 the second of you，可以定位到该题。接着原文提到 “… will be working in the manufacturing line to help develop new products ...”，其中 manufacturing 对应 C 选项 production，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。注意，该句中的 develop new products 与 E 选项的信息存在偏差，可判断 E 选项为干扰项。

questions.title: 3rd person
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 3rd person。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 the third person，可以定位到该题。接着原文提到 “… your main job is to look after the mechanical side ...”，其中 look after，以及随后出现的 checking equipment、doing some repair work 等信息都对应 D 选项 maintenance，可判断 D 选项为正确答案。之后原文提到 “Without your help, many processes like manufacturing or packaging won't work well”，注意该句中的 manufacturing 可与 C 选项形成干扰。

questions.title: 4th person
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 4th person。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 the fourth person，可以定位到该题。接着原文提到 “… will work in the technology department to deal with problems related to software and hardware.”，其中 technology、software、hardware 等信息对应 A 选项 computer，之后原文还提到 laptops，可确认 A 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: 5th person
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 5th person。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 the last of you，可以定位到该题。接着原文提到 “You are expected to keep a long-term relationship with customers ...”，其中 keep a long-term relationship with customers 对应 F 选项 maintaining customer relationship。之后原文还提到 collecting feedback regularly、dealing with customer complaints、enhancing customer satisfaction 等信息，都是 F 选项的具象化体现，可确认 F 选项为正确答案。

transcript.en: Good morning, everyone!
transcript.cn: 大家早上好！

transcript.en: My name's Janet Parker, and I'm the Human Resources Manager of this company.
transcript.cn: 我叫珍妮特·帕克，是这家公司的人力资源经理。

transcript.en: Thank you for joining us and coming to this training session, which I hope will help you quickly learn about our company and know what you will do in your position.
transcript.cn: 感谢大家加入我们并参加这次培训，我希望它能帮助你们迅速了解公司，清楚自己在岗位上要做什么。

transcript.en: First, I'll briefly introduce the history of our company.
transcript.cn: 首先，我来简要介绍一下公司的历史。

transcript.en: We're quite an old company, specializing in electronic toys for more than thirty years.
transcript.cn: 我们是一家历史相当悠久的公司，专门生产电子玩具已有三十多年。

transcript.en: The founder, Ronald Stevenson, got the idea of establishing his own company when he finished his apprenticeship.
transcript.cn: 创始人罗纳德·史蒂文森在完成学徒期时，萌生了创办自己公司的想法。

transcript.en: With the support of his grandfather, he got enough start-up capital and the company was set up and succeeded in a short period.
transcript.cn: 在祖父的支持下，他获得了足够的启动资金，公司得以成立并在短时间内取得成功。

transcript.en: To satisfy the demands of customers, we've expanded the premises considerably over the past years, and the number of staff members has grown up on a regular basis.
transcript.cn: 为满足客户需求，过去几年我们大幅扩建了经营场所，员工人数也一直稳步增长。

transcript.en: Recently we found the sales of our products creeping up very slowly, and we decided to introduce new electronic toys to give that a boost, so we have to look for more workers to help us, and that's the reason why you are here.
transcript.cn: 最近我们发现产品销量增长得非常缓慢，于是决定推出新的电子玩具来促进销售，因此必须招募更多员工来协助我们，这就是你们来到这里的原因。

transcript.en: The first week may be pretty challenging as there will be a lot of new information.
transcript.cn: 第一周可能会很有挑战性，因为会有大量新信息。

transcript.en: But don't worry too much about trying to remember everything.
transcript.cn: 不过，不必过于担心要把一切都记住。

transcript.en: The important thing is to check with someone if you're unsure what to do.
transcript.cn: 重要的是，如果不确定该怎么做，就向别人确认。

transcript.en: According to the contract, you'll be expected to work here for six months.
transcript.cn: 根据合同，你们需要在这里工作六个月。

transcript.en: Some of you asked me if there is a possibility for you to be employed as our full-time member.
transcript.cn: 你们中有人问过我，是否有机会成为我们的全职员工。

transcript.en: I can't give you a definite answer now.
transcript.cn: 我现在无法给出明确答复。

transcript.en: It'll depend on your job performance during the employment and whether we have job vacancies then.
transcript.cn: 这将取决于你们受雇期间的工作表现，以及届时我们是否有职位空缺。

transcript.en: About two hundred full-time staff members in our company, seventy percent of whom are responsible for sending out goods, while the minority of jobs are in the manufacturing line.
transcript.cn: 我们公司大约有两百名全职员工，其中百分之七十负责发货，只有少数岗位在生产线上。

transcript.en: You will work in different departments according to our needs, which I'll explain to you in detail later.
transcript.cn: 你们将根据我们的需要在不同部门工作，稍后我会详细说明。

transcript.en: Working in our company, you'll be impressed by our modern machinery and production methods, advanced skills and friendly working atmosphere.
transcript.cn: 在我们公司工作，现代化的机械和生产方式、先进的技术以及友好的工作氛围都会给你们留下深刻印象。

transcript.en: However, I must tell you one thing which is not so pleasant.
transcript.cn: 不过，我必须告诉你们一件不太愉快的事。

transcript.en: Because the manager of the payroll department is on sick leave these days, I'm afraid that you won't get paid on time this month.
transcript.cn: 由于薪资部门经理这几天请病假，恐怕你们这个月无法按时领到工资。

transcript.en: But don't worry too much; it'll be solved very soon.
transcript.cn: 但不必太担心，这个问题很快就会解决。

transcript.en: In the second part of this training session, I'll explain what you will be responsible for in the following working days.
transcript.cn: 在本次培训的第二部分，我会说明接下来几个工作日里你们各自负责的工作。

transcript.en: There are five positions and you'll be working in different departments.
transcript.cn: 共有五个岗位，你们将在不同部门工作。

transcript.en: As I've mentioned before, we're in urgent need of someone who can deal with the salary problem, so our staff can get paid as soon as possible, and that's the primary job for the first person.
transcript.cn: 正如我之前提到的，我们急需有人处理薪资问题，让员工尽快拿到工资，这就是第一位员工的首要任务。

transcript.en: Then the second of you will be working in the manufacturing line to help develop new products, and I'm sure you will be pretty busy in the following days.
transcript.cn: 第二位将在生产线上工作，协助开发新产品，我相信接下来几天你会非常忙。

transcript.en: The third person will work on the sixth floor, and your main job is to look after the mechanical side.
transcript.cn: 第三位将在六楼工作，主要职责是负责机械方面的事务。

transcript.en: It includes checking equipment daily to ensure they are working correctly and doing some repair work if necessary.
transcript.cn: 这包括每天检查设备以确保其正常运转，并在必要时进行维修。

transcript.en: Without your help, many processes like manufacturing or packaging won't work well.
transcript.cn: 没有你的帮助，生产或包装等许多流程都无法顺利运行。

transcript.en: Then, the fourth person will work in the technology department to deal with problems related to software and hardware.
transcript.cn: 第四位将在技术部门工作，处理与软件和硬件有关的问题。

transcript.en: We're getting new laptops next week, and you must install new programs.
transcript.cn: 我们下周会购置新笔记本电脑，你必须安装新程序。

transcript.en: And the last of you will work in the after-sale service department.
transcript.cn: 最后一位将在售后服务部门工作。

transcript.en: You are expected to keep a long-term relationship with customers, including collecting feedback regularly, dealing with customer complaints, enhancing customer satisfaction etc.
transcript.cn: 你需要与客户维持长期关系，包括定期收集反馈、处理客户投诉、提高客户满意度等。

transcript.en: OK, now that's all I want to share with you today.
transcript.cn: 好了，这就是我今天想和大家分享的全部内容。

transcript.en: Are there any questions ...
transcript.cn: 有什么问题吗……

groups.title: Project on Sports Shoes
groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Choose the correct answer, A, B or C.

questions.title: What did the students get from the website?

options.title: B
options.content: ideas to fulfil the project requirements

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 website；提示词为 students、get。
题目解析：开篇在稍作背景介绍后，定位词由女生引出，接着她提到 “… where I found some articles on sports shoes ...”，与 C 选项信息一致，其中 articles 是 information 的具象化体现。随后男生表示同意 “Yes, I searched for pictures of sports shoes online ...”，其中 pictures 也是 C 选项 information 的具象化体现，online 对应题干的 website，可确认 C 选项为正确答案。注意，A 选项 advice from others 并非是从网上获取的信息，属于干扰项；女生有提到 B 选项的 ideas，但她并未提到 fulfil the project requirements，可判断该选项信息不完整，为干扰项。

questions.title: What did Martin use as an example of the research?

options.title: A
options.content: He downloaded a diagram from the internet.

options.title: B
options.content: He took apart his old shoes.

options.title: C
options.content: He borrowed a sample from the tutor.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 example；提示词为 Martin。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，女生先提出 borrow a sample from the tutor，与 C 选项信息一致，但随后被男生否定 “That was my first thought. But I realized maybe the tutor wanted us to do our own thing, not copy.”，可排除 C 选项。男生提到 “Then I cut off my old sports shoes and separated them into different parts ...”，与 B 选项信息一致，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。最后男生还提到 “Then I drew a diagram ...”，说明 diagram 并不是在网上下载的，与 A 选项信息不一致，可判断其为干扰项。

questions.title: According to Martin, what other factors should be considered while designing shoes?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 designing shoes；提示词为 Martin、other factors。
题目解析：定位词以 designers 的形式出现，不难听出。原文先后提到 designers these days use better raw materials 和 they also take into account the factor of foot shape，说明设计师已经考虑到 raw materials 和 foot shape 这两个因素了，可判断 A、C 选项为干扰项。最后男生提到 “But they've ignored the foot movement ...”，其中 ignored 对应题干的 should be considered，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: What proportion of the essay does Martin suggest Rosie do?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 essay；提示词为 proportion、Martin suggest Rosie。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，男生提到 “… I think you can mainly finish the written sections of the project ...”，其中 written sections 即为 essay；mainly 对应 B 选项的 almost all，因此 B 选项为正确答案。男生随后还提到 “… I can take care of every detail of the presentation.”，其中 every detail 指的是 presentation 的细节，而非 essay 的，此外这也不是女生负责的任务，可判断 C 选项为干扰项。A 选项在原文未提及。

questions.title: What will Martin and Rosie do this weekend?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 this weekend；提示词为 Martin and Rosie、this weekend。
题目解析：上一题结束后，通过提问 “What do we need to do next?” 可以定位到该题。先听到 according to the plan，A 选项 carry out a plan 信息有偏差，可排除 A 选项。接着听到 “… we'll have to finish the questionnaire left by the tutor, and I think we can handle it this weekend,”，其中 questionnaire 对应 C 选项的 questions，this weekend 原词重现，因此 C 选项为正确答案。之后男生提出疑问 “Do we have to do some face-to-face interviews?”，但随即被女生否定 “The tutor said that would be for the next assignment, not this one.”，可判断 B 选项为干扰项。

questions.title: What help can the students get from the tutor?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 help、students、tutor。
题目解析：本题定位信息不明显。上一题结束后，原文提到 “… in the last tutorial, the tutor advised us ...”，部分考生听到 tutorial 和 advised 可能会误选 B、C 选项。注意，tutorial 和 advice 并非学生可以从导师那里获得的帮助，这两个选项皆为干扰项。接着男生提到 “But I'll also need a whiteboard ...”，其中 whiteboard 对应 A 选项的 equipment，随后女生回应 “We can ask our tutor for help. He might have one and can lend it to us,”，其中 tutor、help 都是题干原词，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。

groups.question_title: What decision do the students make about each of the following parts of their essay?
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What decision do the students make about each of the following parts of their essay?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

questions.title: Summary
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 summary。
题目解析：定位词由男生引出 “I think the summary gives a brief and precise description of the project's main ideas.”，句中没有与选项相关的信息。继续往下听，听到女生提到 “I'd like to put it at the end of the essay”，其中 at the end of 与 F 选项的 the last part 信息一致，接着男生也表示同意，可判断 F 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Result
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 result。
题目解析：定位词仍由男生引出 “I think the result part describes in detail what the research has achieved.”，其中 describes in detail 与 C 选项 give a detailed example 信息存在偏差，可排除 C 选项。随后男生通过转折词引出答案句 “… it should go directly behind the part of the research ...”，女生表示同意 “Yes, I think that'd be better. I'll move this part.”，其中 move this part 与 D 选项 change to another part 信息一致，可确认 D 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Conclusion
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 conclusion。
题目解析：上一题结束后，定位词原词重现。接着女生先后提到 too many technical terms、struggle with，随后男生提到 not ... familiar with，这些信息都表明难度大，学生不容易理解，对应 E 选项。最后女生提到 “So I think we could replace them with straightforward explanations,”，其中 straightforward 对应 E 选项的 simple，可确认 E 选项为正确答案。

questions.title: Discussion
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 discussion。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，男生先提到 “… I like this part as it presents a series of questions that we can talk about with other students after the presentation.”，句中没有与选项相关的信息。继续往下听，听到女生提到 “… but I think the questions look messy and need to be better sequenced. Maybe we could arrange them in a list.”，其中 better sequenced、arrange them in a list 对应 A 选项 change the layout，可确认 A 选项为正确答案。

transcript.en: Rosie: OK, Martin.
transcript.cn: 好的，马丁。

transcript.en: Rosie: Before we plan our presentation for the project, let's discuss what we've done so far.
transcript.cn: 在规划项目展示之前，我们先讨论一下目前已经完成的工作吧。

transcript.en: Martin: OK, Rosie.
transcript.cn: 好的，罗茜。

transcript.en: Martin: When the tutor gave us the theme of sports shoes to base our project on, I was not all that interested.
transcript.cn: 导师让我们以运动鞋为主题开展项目时，我其实没什么兴趣。

transcript.en: Martin: But I'm starting to get into it.
transcript.cn: 但我现在开始对它感兴趣了。

transcript.en: Martin: I've pretty much finished the research stage.
transcript.cn: 我的研究阶段差不多已经完成了。

transcript.en: Rosie: Me too.
transcript.cn: 我也是。

transcript.en: Rosie: At first, I had no idea what to write for the essay, so I asked some senior students for advice, and they gave me some websites to look at, where I found some articles on sports shoes and got some ideas.
transcript.cn: 一开始我不知道论文该写什么，所以向几位高年级学生请教，他们给了我一些网站，我在上面找到了几篇关于运动鞋的文章，也获得了一些思路。

transcript.en: Martin: Yes, I searched for pictures of sports shoes online and applied them in my research.
transcript.cn: 是的，我在网上搜索了运动鞋的图片，并把它们运用到研究中。

transcript.en: Martin: It's beneficial.
transcript.cn: 这很有帮助。

transcript.en: Rosie: Right.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Martin: According to the project's requirements, we have to find an example for the research, and I've just completed this part.
transcript.cn: 根据项目要求，我们必须为研究找一个实例，而我刚刚完成了这部分。

transcript.en: Rosie: What example do you use?
transcript.cn: 你用了什么实例？

transcript.en: Rosie: You said you'd like to borrow a sample from the tutor.
transcript.cn: 你之前说想向导师借一个样品。

transcript.en: Martin: That was my first thought.
transcript.cn: 那是我最初的想法。

transcript.en: Martin: But I realized maybe the tutor wanted us to do our own thing, not copy.
transcript.cn: 但我意识到，导师或许希望我们做出自己的东西，而不是照搬。

transcript.en: Martin: Then I cut off my old sports shoes and separated them into different parts so that I could make a careful study of the internal structure.
transcript.cn: 于是我把自己的旧运动鞋剪开，拆分成不同部分，以便仔细研究其内部结构。

transcript.en: Rosie: It's a good idea.
transcript.cn: 这是个好主意。

transcript.en: Martin: Then I drew a diagram which could show precisely each part of the shoes.
transcript.cn: 然后我画了一张示意图，可以精确展示鞋子的各个部分。

transcript.en: Martin: Through the research, I've found that sports shoes nowadays have significantly improved.
transcript.cn: 通过研究，我发现如今的运动鞋已经有了显著改进。

transcript.en: Rosie: What do you mean?
transcript.cn: 你是什么意思？

transcript.en: Martin: For example, designers these days use better raw materials, which are not only durable but also make the skin of feet breathe more easily.
transcript.cn: 例如，现在的设计师采用了更好的原材料，这些材料不仅耐用，还能让脚部皮肤更容易透气。

transcript.en: Martin: Moreover, they also take into account the factor of foot shape.
transcript.cn: 此外，他们也考虑到了脚型因素。

transcript.en: Martin: But they've ignored the foot movement, which is essential for sportspeople.
transcript.cn: 但他们忽略了脚部运动，而这对运动员来说至关重要。

transcript.en: Rosie: Interesting!
transcript.cn: 真有意思！

transcript.en: Rosie: I think I can add this to the essay.
transcript.cn: 我觉得可以把这点加进论文。

transcript.en: Martin: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Martin: Given that you've got most ideas for the essay so far, I think you can mainly finish the written sections of the project, and I can take care of every detail of the presentation.
transcript.cn: 既然你目前已经有了论文的大部分思路，我想你可以主要负责完成项目的书面部分，而我可以负责展示的所有细节。

transcript.en: Martin: Does this appeal to you?
transcript.cn: 你觉得这样安排怎么样？

transcript.en: Rosie: I'm OK with that.
transcript.cn: 我没问题。

transcript.en: Rosie: I prefer written work to giving a presentation.
transcript.cn: 比起做展示，我更喜欢书面工作。

transcript.en: Martin: That's good.
transcript.cn: 那很好。

transcript.en: Martin: What do we need to do next?
transcript.cn: 接下来我们需要做什么？

transcript.en: Rosie: Well, according to the plan, we will give a presentation next Wednesday.
transcript.cn: 嗯，按照计划，我们将在下周三做展示。

transcript.en: Rosie: And before that, we'll have to finish the questionnaire left by the tutor, and I think we can handle it this weekend.
transcript.cn: 在此之前，我们必须完成导师留下的问卷，我想这个周末就能处理好。

transcript.en: Martin: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Martin: Do we have to do some face-to-face interviews?
transcript.cn: 我们必须做一些面对面访谈吗？

transcript.en: Rosie: The tutor said that would be for the next assignment, not this one.
transcript.cn: 导师说那是下一项作业的内容，不是这一次的。

transcript.en: Martin: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Martin: Then about the presentation, I remember in the last tutorial, the tutor advised us to use some slides to make the presentation more attractive.
transcript.cn: 至于展示，我记得上次辅导课上，导师建议我们使用一些幻灯片，让展示更有吸引力。

transcript.en: Martin: But I'll also need a whiteboard because I like writing or drawing when necessary.
transcript.cn: 不过我还需要一块白板，因为必要时我喜欢写写画画。

transcript.en: Rosie: We can ask our tutor for help.
transcript.cn: 我们可以请导师帮忙。

transcript.en: Rosie: He might have one and can lend it to us.
transcript.cn: 他可能有一块，可以借给我们。

transcript.en: Martin: Right.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Rosie: Well, Martin, let's go through the draft of the essay together and see whether there're any changes we have to make.
transcript.cn: 好了，马丁，我们一起过一遍论文草稿，看看是否有需要修改的地方。

transcript.en: Martin: OK, Rosie.
transcript.cn: 好的，罗茜。

transcript.en: Martin: I think the summary gives a brief and precise description of the project's main ideas.
transcript.cn: 我认为摘要对项目的主要观点作了简明准确的概述。

transcript.en: Martin: Where will it go?
transcript.cn: 它要放在哪里？

transcript.en: Rosie: I'd like to put it at the end of the essay.
transcript.cn: 我想把它放在论文末尾。

transcript.en: Rosie: What do you think?
transcript.cn: 你觉得呢？

transcript.en: Martin: Good idea.
transcript.cn: 好主意。

transcript.en: Martin: And I think the result part describes in detail what the research has achieved.
transcript.cn: 而且我认为结果部分详细描述了这项研究取得的成果。

transcript.en: Martin: But I'm wondering whether it should go directly behind the part of the research, as they are closely related.
transcript.cn: 但我在想，它是否应该紧接在研究部分之后，因为两者关系密切。

transcript.en: Rosie: Yes, I think that'd be better.
transcript.cn: 是的，我觉得那样会更好。

transcript.en: Rosie: I'll move this part.
transcript.cn: 我会把这部分移过去。

transcript.en: Rosie: Then the conclusion, I'm wondering whether I've used too many technical terms, which other students might struggle with.
transcript.cn: 然后是结论部分，我在想自己是不是用了太多专业术语，其他学生可能难以理解。

transcript.en: Martin: You're right.
transcript.cn: 你说得对。

transcript.en: Martin: Not everyone will be familiar with the subject.
transcript.cn: 并非所有人都熟悉这个主题。

transcript.en: Rosie: So I think we could replace them with straightforward explanations.
transcript.cn: 所以我觉得可以用浅显易懂的解释来替换这些术语。

transcript.en: Martin: I agree.
transcript.cn: 我同意。

transcript.en: Martin: Lastly, the discussion, I like this part as it presents a series of questions that we can talk about with other students after the presentation.
transcript.cn: 最后是讨论部分，我喜欢这部分，因为它提出了一系列问题，展示结束后我们可以和其他学生一起讨论。

transcript.en: Rosie: Yes, but I think the questions look messy and need to be better sequenced.
transcript.cn: 是的，但我觉得这些问题看起来很杂乱，需要更合理地排序。

transcript.en: Rosie: Maybe we could arrange them in a list.
transcript.cn: 也许可以把它们整理成一个列表。

transcript.en: Martin: OK, that'd be better.
transcript.cn: 好的，那样会更好。

transcript.en: Rosie: All right.
transcript.cn: 好了。

transcript.en: Rosie: That's all about the essay.
transcript.cn: 关于论文的内容就这些。

transcript.en: Rosie: Now let's talk about the presentation ...
transcript.cn: 现在我们来谈谈展示……

groups.title: African Clawed Frog (Xenopus Laevis)
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Description: • They have no tongue or external ears. • Their skin is $31$. • The mucus on their skin is for $32$. • Male frogs are $33$ the size of female frogs. Habitats: • They prefer $34$ water. • They live in oases or glacier-covered lakes. • They live in a condition with a high level of $35$. • In the condition of drought, they can survive in $36$ for a long period of time. Diet: • They use the sense of $37$ to capture food. • They use their claws to tear apart ample food. • They use their front feet and a unique $38$ system when feeding. Other information: • People in some states of the USA regard them as $39$. • The mucus on their skin has $40$ properties.

questions.title: Their skin is 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 skin；提示词为 is，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：开篇在稍作背景介绍后，小标题 description 和上层信息 no tongue or external ears 先后出现，提示定位到该题。接着原文提到 African clawed frogs have smooth skin，其中 smooth 搭配 skin 出现，符合题干要求，可判断其为正确答案。注意，之后原文提到 unlike the toad, whose skin always has a rough texture，根据否定词 unlike，可排除 rough（toad 的皮肤特点）。

questions.title: The mucus on their skin is for 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 mucus；提示词为 for，此处应填名词。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，原文提到 which can give their sensitive skin protection，其中 which 指代前面的 mucus，可判断 protection 为正确答案。本题难度不大。

questions.title: Male frogs are 【   】 the size of female frogs.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 male frogs、female frogs；提示词为 are、size，此处应填表示倍数的词。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，根据题干要求抓取表示倍数的词。原文提到 male frogs are about half as big as females，其中 big 对应题干的 size，可判断 half 为正确答案。

questions.title: They prefer 【   】 water.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 habitats；提示词为 prefer、water，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：小标题 habitats 原词重现后，可以听到题干的 water 出现（they live in water all their life），但其中并无修饰 water 的词。继续往下听，听到 they particularly like still water，其中 particularly like 对应题干的 prefer，still 修饰 water，可判断其为正确答案。注意，之后听到 instead of fast-flowing streams，根据否定词组 instead of 可判断 fast-flowing 为干扰信息。

questions.title: They live in a condition with a high level of 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 oases、glacier-covered lakes；提示词为 live、high level of，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题与上一题之间间隔时间较长，考生需耐心听下去。当听到上层信息 oases 和 glacier-covered lakes 原词重现时，可以定位到该题，准备抓取答案词。之后听到 “Besides, they can even survive in water with high salt levels.”，其中 survive in 对应题干的 live in；water 对应 condition，可判断 salt 符合题干要求，为正确答案。

questions.title: In the condition of drought, they can survive in 【   】 for a long period of time.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 drought；提示词为 survive in、a long period of time，此处应填名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，可以听到 in dry weather，其中 dry 对应题干的 drought，提示定位到该题。接着听到 “… African clawed frogs always burrow themselves into mud, where they can survive for more than a year”，其中 more than a year 对应题干的 a long period of time，可判断 mud 符合题干要求，为正确答案。

questions.title: They use the sense of 【   】 to capture food.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 diet；提示词为 sense of、capture food，此处应填名词，表示某种感觉官能。
题目解析：听力录音稍作停顿后，小标题 diet 原词重现，提示定位到该题。根据题干要求直接抓取感官类名词，原文提到 “African clawed frogs depend on the sense of smell to detect their prey.”，其中 detect their prey 对应题干的 capture food；sense of smell 与题干结构一致，可判断 smell 为正确答案。

questions.title: They use their front feet and a unique 【   】 system when feeding.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 front feet；提示词为 unique、system、feeding，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：定位词 front feet 原词重现后，答案句并没有随后出现，而是在解释 front feet 的作用。接着听到 meanwhile 一词，考生可通过这个并列信号词辅助定位。随后听到 a distinctive pump system is used to draw or suck food in their mouths，其中 distinctive 对应题干的 unique，draw or suck food in their mouths 对应 feeding，可判断 pump 前后信息与题干相符，为正确答案。本题难度较大。

questions.title: People in some states of the USA regard them as 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 states of the USA；提示词为 regard as，此处应填名词或形容词。
题目解析：本题与上一题之间间隔时间较长，考生需耐心听下去。当听到 “… states of America like California, Washington, Arizona etc. ...” 时，可以定位到该题。随后原文提到 “… where African clawed frogs have been seen as a type of pest,”，其中 see as 对应题干的 regard as，可判断 pest 符合题干要求，为正确答案。

questions.title: The mucus on their skin has 【   】 properties.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 mucus、skin；提示词为 has、properties，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：定位词原词重现后，原文提到 “… the skin of African clawed frogs is covered with mucus, which has properties of antibiotic”，其中 antibiotic 搭配 properties，符合题干要求，为正确答案。本题所考查的词汇难度较大。

transcript.en: Today I'd like to look at another type of amphibian - the African clawed frog, whose Latin name is Xenopus laevis.
transcript.cn: 今天我想介绍另一种两栖动物——非洲爪蟾，它的拉丁学名是 Xenopus laevis。

transcript.en: Its name is derived from the three short claws on each hind foot, which it uses to tear apart its food.
transcript.cn: 它的名字源于每只后足上的三只短爪，它会用这些爪子撕开食物。

transcript.en: The Latin word Xenopus means 'strange foot' and laevis means 'smooth'.
transcript.cn: 拉丁词 Xenopus 意为“奇异的脚”，laevis 意为“光滑的”。

transcript.en: I'll start with a general description.
transcript.cn: 我先做一个总体介绍。

transcript.en: The African clawed frog is a species of frog that can be commonly found in the eastern and southern parts of Africa.
transcript.cn: 非洲爪蟾是一种常见于非洲东部和南部的蛙类。

transcript.en: They have a flattened head and body but no tongue or external ears.
transcript.cn: 它们的头部和身体扁平，但没有舌头和外耳。

transcript.en: As the Latin name suggests, African clawed frogs have smooth skin, unlike the toad, whose skin always has a rough texture.
transcript.cn: 正如它的拉丁学名所示，非洲爪蟾皮肤光滑，不像蟾蜍的皮肤总是质地粗糙。

transcript.en: The body of African clawed frogs is covered with mucus, which can give their sensitive skin protection, thus playing an indispensable role in their survival.
transcript.cn: 非洲爪蟾全身覆盖着黏液，可以保护其敏感的皮肤，因此对它们的生存起着不可或缺的作用。

transcript.en: Male and female African clawed frogs can be easily distinguished through several characteristics, and the major one is their size.
transcript.cn: 通过几个特征可以轻易区分雌雄非洲爪蟾，其中最主要的特征是体型。

transcript.en: Generally, male frogs are about half as big as females.
transcript.cn: 一般来说，雄蛙的体型约为雌蛙的一半。

transcript.en: Specifically, female frogs are usually 4.5 inches long, weighing around 7 ounces, while males are generally 2.5 inches long and 2 ounces heavy.
transcript.cn: 具体而言，雌蛙通常长 4.5 英寸、重约 7 盎司，而雄蛙一般长 2.5 英寸、重 2 盎司。

transcript.en: Then let's turn to their habitats.
transcript.cn: 接下来我们来看看它们的栖息地。

transcript.en: African clawed frogs are entirely aquatic frogs, so they live in water all their life, whether they are tadpoles or adult frogs.
transcript.cn: 非洲爪蟾是完全水生的蛙类，因此无论是蝌蚪还是成蛙，一生都生活在水中。

transcript.en: Although they can live in a wide range of freshwater environments, they particularly like still water instead of fast-flowing streams.
transcript.cn: 虽然它们可以生活在多种淡水环境中，但尤其喜欢静水，而不是水流湍急的溪流。

transcript.en: During the summer, African clawed frogs are mainly found in warm rivers, and during the rainy season, they tend to move into flooded forests.
transcript.cn: 夏季，非洲爪蟾主要生活在温暖的河流中；到了雨季，它们往往会迁入被洪水淹没的森林。

transcript.en: In the wild, African clawed frogs are highly adaptable to changes in the environment around them, whether in oases or glacier-covered lakes.
transcript.cn: 在野外，无论是在绿洲还是冰川覆盖的湖泊中，非洲爪蟾对周围环境的变化都有很强的适应能力。

transcript.en: Besides, they can even survive in water with high salt levels.
transcript.cn: 此外，它们甚至能在含盐量高的水中生存。

transcript.en: It is because African clawed frogs have a unique physiological mechanism in their body that could automatically regulate the balance of water inside and outside their body.
transcript.cn: 这是因为非洲爪蟾体内有一种独特的生理机制，能够自动调节体内外的水分平衡。

transcript.en: In dry weather, African clawed frogs always burrow themselves into mud, where they can survive for more than a year.
transcript.cn: 天气干旱时，非洲爪蟾总会钻入泥中，并能在那里存活一年以上。

transcript.en: They are incredibly hardy and long-lived, with an average lifespan of five to fifteen years.
transcript.cn: 它们生命力极强且寿命很长，平均寿命为五至十五年。

transcript.en: Some are even recorded to have lived for twenty to twenty-five years.
transcript.cn: 据记录，有些甚至活了二十至二十五年。

transcript.en: However, African clawed frogs are susceptible to the pollution of the water even though they are not listed as endangered species.
transcript.cn: 不过，尽管非洲爪蟾未被列为濒危物种，它们却很容易受到水污染的影响。

transcript.en: Now, let's talk about the diet of African clawed frogs.
transcript.cn: 现在，我们来谈谈非洲爪蟾的饮食。

transcript.en: They are carnivores that like hunting insects, spiders, worms, fish etc.
transcript.cn: 它们是肉食动物，喜欢捕食昆虫、蜘蛛、蠕虫和鱼类等。

transcript.en: African clawed frogs depend on the sense of smell to detect their prey.
transcript.cn: 非洲爪蟾依靠嗅觉探测猎物。

transcript.en: They first use the claws on their hind feet to tear pieces of ample food, then the front feet, which are not webbed, play an important role in feeding.
transcript.cn: 它们先用后足的爪子把大块食物撕碎，然后由没有蹼的前足在进食过程中发挥重要作用。

transcript.en: African clawed frogs use their front feet to push food into their mouths, and meanwhile, a distinctive pump system is used to draw or suck food in their mouths.
transcript.cn: 非洲爪蟾用前足把食物推入口中，与此同时，它们会利用一种独特的泵吸系统将食物吸入口中。

transcript.en: So we can see African clawed frogs are voracious predators and can quickly adapt to many habitats.
transcript.cn: 由此可见，非洲爪蟾是贪食的捕食者，并且能迅速适应多种栖息地。

transcript.en: Therefore, in some parts of the world, African clawed frogs act like invasive species which will negatively affect other species in the freshwater ecosystems.
transcript.cn: 因此，在世界某些地区，非洲爪蟾如同入侵物种，会对淡水生态系统中的其他物种产生不利影响。

transcript.en: The evidence shows that they have devastated the populations of native frogs and other creatures by eating their young.
transcript.cn: 证据表明，它们通过捕食本地蛙类及其他生物的幼体，严重破坏了这些种群。

transcript.en: As a result, African clawed frogs are illegal to own, transport, or sell without any permit in some states of America like California, Washington, Arizona etc., where African clawed frogs have been seen as a type of pest.
transcript.cn: 因此，在美国的加利福尼亚州、华盛顿州和亚利桑那州等一些将非洲爪蟾视为有害生物的州，未经许可持有、运输或出售非洲爪蟾均属违法。

transcript.en: However, it's legal to own African clawed frogs in Canada.
transcript.cn: 不过，在加拿大持有非洲爪蟾是合法的。

transcript.en: But not many people know that African clawed frogs are also used in medicine.
transcript.cn: 但很少有人知道，非洲爪蟾也被用于医药领域。

transcript.en: As we mentioned before, the skin of African clawed frogs is covered with mucus, which has properties of antibiotic.
transcript.cn: 正如我们之前提到的，非洲爪蟾的皮肤覆盖着具有抗菌特性的黏液。

transcript.en: This chemical can help kill bacteria and cure the infection and is widely used by both the medical profession and the general public.
transcript.cn: 这种化学物质可以帮助杀灭细菌、治疗感染，医疗专业人员和普通大众都广泛使用它。

transcript.en: So we shouldn't ignore the medical value of African clawed frogs.
transcript.cn: 所以我们不应忽视非洲爪蟾的医学价值。
